tickerText: [TENNIS] Wimbledon 2026 Day 2 at the All England Club Tuesday, June 30: defending champion Jannik Sinner survived a five-set scare against Miomir Kecmanovic on Day 1 and now eyes Nuno Borges in Round 2, while Centre Court hosts Serena Williams' long-awaited return against Australia's Maya Joint alongside Iga Świątek vs Taylor Townsend, Alexander Zverev, Taylor Fritz, and Ben Shelton as eight seeds fell across Monday's slippery opening slate • [SQUASH] HCL Squash PSA Challenger Tour in Mumbai advances to Round 2 Tuesday at Juhu Vile Parle Gymkhana — 48 players from 15 countries contest the $30,000 combined purse through July 3 as the PSA World Tour's summer slate heats up in India after Diego Elias and Hania El Hammamy closed the 2025-26 season at the Tour Finals in Paris • [BADMINTON] YONEX Canada Open 2026 opens Tuesday at Markham Pan Am Centre in Ontario — qualification rounds begin at 9am local ahead of main-draw Round of 32 play at the $250K BWF Super 300, days after Su Li Yang and Line Christophersen lifted US Open titles in Fullerton and as Malaysian shuttler Ng Tze Yong exits the national team effective June 30 • [PICKLEBALL] PPA Asia 500 Tokyo Open arrives July 1–4 as the Carvana PPA Tour's international calendar rolls through Asia — the stop follows June's China Open and Portland Challenger while Singapore, Ho Chi Minh City, and Shenzhen 500s loom as Anna Leigh Waters lands on the TIME100 Sports List and mid-year rankings reset the chase for Veolia National Championships berths in Cary • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT United States Smash 2026 enters Round of 32 Tuesday at the Ontario Convention Center — defending champion Wang Chuqin swept Zhou Qihao and Wang Manyu crushed Irene Yeoh, but No. 7 Lin Yun-ju fell to Kuo Guan-hong as early upsets shake the $1.55M Grand Smash field through the July 5 finals

newsItems.title: Serena Williams Returns as Wimbledon Day 2 Opens Across London
newsItems.excerpt: The Championships enter Day 2 on Tuesday, June 30, with first-round play continuing across SW19 after a dramatic opening Monday. Defending men's champion Jannik Sinner rallied from a set down to beat Miomir Kecmanovic 4-6, 6-3, 6-7 (6-8), 6-2, 6-3 in three hours and 30 minutes, surviving two falls on the grass and 52 unforced errors to avoid becoming the first defending men's champion to lose in Round 1 since Lleyton Hewitt in 2003. Eight seeds fell on Day 1, including Casper Ruud, Andrey Rublev, and French Open finalist Maja Chwalinska. Tuesday's marquee Centre Court slate features seven-time Wimbledon winner Serena Williams against Australia's Maya Joint, defending women's champion Iga Świątek against Taylor Townsend, and top seeds Alexander Zverev, Taylor Fritz, and Ben Shelton across the grounds.

newsItems.title: Canada Open Begins in Markham as BWF World Tour Hits Ontario
newsItems.excerpt: The 2026 YONEX Canada Open opened Tuesday, June 30, at the Markham Pan Am Centre in Ontario, marking the BWF World Tour's return to Canada for a six-day Super 300 event with a $250,000 prize pool. Qualification rounds across all five disciplines began at 9:00am local time, with main-draw Round of 32 matches scheduled for the afternoon session. The tournament runs through Sunday, July 5, and follows last week's US Open in Fullerton, where Su Li Yang and Denmark's Line Christophersen claimed singles titles. Off court, Malaysian men's singles player Ng Tze Yong left the national team effective June 30 after reaching a mutual agreement with the Badminton Association of Malaysia to continue his career as an independent shuttler following injury setbacks.

newsItems.title: Tokyo Open Kicks Off July as PPA Tour Charts Record Asian Season
newsItems.excerpt: The Carvana PPA Tour's global calendar turns to Tokyo next as the PPA Asia 500 Tokyo Open runs July 1–4, the latest stop in a record-setting Asian slate that already featured the China Open in Beijing and will continue through Singapore, Ho Chi Minh City, and Shenzhen. The tour's 2026–2027 schedule expands further with PPA Canada and PPA Italy debuts, a $500,000 Australian Pickleball Open, and the Veolia Pickleball National Championships in Cary, North Carolina, opening August 31. Anna Leigh Waters was named to the TIME100 Sports List and featured on CBS News Sunday Morning as mid-year player rankings highlighted the depth chasing Ben Johns and Waters atop the unified global standings.

newsItems.title: Lin Yun-ju Upset Headlines WTT US Smash as Wang Chuqin Defends
newsItems.excerpt: Main-draw action at the WTT United States Smash 2026 continued at the Ontario Convention Center in California as the $1.55 million Grand Smash moved toward Round of 32 play. Defending men's champion and world No. 1 Wang Chuqin opened his title defense with an 11-6, 15-13, 11-3 win over teammate Zhou Qihao, while Olympic champion Wang Manyu dispatched 15-year-old American Irene Yeoh 11-5, 11-3, 11-4. The biggest early shock came when No. 7 seed Lin Yun-ju of Chinese Taipei lost in five games to teammate Kuo Guan-hong. Lin Shidong and Chen Yuanyu also reached the last 32, but China-U.S. doubles pairings featuring Kanak Jha were eliminated in the first round as the tournament runs through July 5.
